decision.scope: Non-notebook-first and provider-native source variants outside the documented Fabric Lakehouse stable surface.
decision.reason: The Fabric stable-final claim covers contract-generated Notebook execution over the validated Lakehouse, shortcut, JDBC, HTTP/REST, Kafka-compatible and Iceberg table shortcut paths. Private-network shortcut variants, managed identity/OAuth object-storage credentials, Delta Sharing client materialization, direct OneLake Iceberg REST catalog reads and native Fabric Real-Time/Eventstream routing require separate runtime mappings and evidence, and must remain REVIEW_REQUIRED rather than silently widening the stable surface.

covered_by_f11: authenticated REST Basic/bearer/API-key/OAuth with Key Vault placeholders
authenticated HTTP JSON Basic/bearer/API-key with Key Vault placeholders
authenticated HTTP CSV Basic/bearer/API-key with Key Vault placeholders
SQL Server JDBC with Key Vault placeholder credentials
PostgreSQL JDBC with Key Vault placeholder credentials
public Azure Blob CSV through Fabric-readable runtime path
private Azure Blob CSV with Key Vault-backed storage account key
external Google Cloud Storage shortcut reads
ADLS Gen2 Iceberg table shortcut reads
Amazon S3 Iceberg table shortcut reads
Google Cloud Storage Iceberg table shortcut reads
Azure Event Hubs Kafka-compatible available-now catch-up

excluded_from_stable_final.planner_behavior: Keep REVIEW_REQUIRED until Fabric private endpoint/network isolation, connection binding and evidence semantics are validated per provider.
Keep REVIEW_REQUIRED until Fabric connection credential semantics and Notebook runtime access are certified for each object-store provider.
Keep REVIEW_REQUIRED until dependency, credential and materialization behavior are validated in Fabric.
Keep REVIEW_REQUIRED until direct OneLake Iceberg REST catalog reads are mapped to ContractForge source semantics. The stable surface uses Fabric Iceberg table shortcuts instead.

Keep REVIEW_REQUIRED until native Fabric Real-Time/Eventstream routing is mapped to bounded or available-now contract semantics. The stable surface uses Kafka-compatible bounded and available-now paths.
Keep REVIEW_REQUIRED until driver, network and credential behavior are validated per dialect.
Keep outside the stable claim because OAuth is not currently part of the HTTP-file source vocabulary.
